analysis.summary: The technician logged into a Windows server (ORX-DC-TS-04) to troubleshoot a Report Server (SSRS) issue. They attempted to access a report URL, which failed with a database connection error. The technician briefly reviewed IIS settings and then searched for troubleshooting information on Microsoft Learn.
actions_taken: Logged into the server as Administrator.
Opened Google Chrome and navigated to a local report server URL (`rnix-dc-ts-04/reports/browse/My_Reports`).
Observed a prolonged loading state, followed by an error indicating the report server could not connect to its database.
Opened Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager and navigated to the 'Default Web Site' configuration.
Searched on Microsoft Learn for 'Configure a report server database connection (Report Server Configuration Manager)' related to the error.
Reviewed the Microsoft Learn article briefly.
issues_observed: The report server URL (`rnix-dc-ts-04/reports/browse/My_Reports`) experienced a very long loading time.

The report server displayed an error: 'Not available. Administrator to resolve the issue. System administrators: The report server can't connect to its database. Make sure the database is running and accessible. You can also check the report server trace log for details.'
notable_events: The report server URL was stuck loading for approximately 1 minute and 29 seconds before displaying an error.
There was a significant idle period of 2 minutes and 04 seconds after the error message appeared before the technician initiated a search for a solution.
concerns: Extended idle time (2 minutes 04 seconds) after an error message appeared, which could indicate a lack of immediate action or communication.

stuck_details: After the report server error appeared at 2:15, the technician remained on the error page without any mouse or keyboard interaction for 2 minutes and 04 seconds, suggesting they were stuck or waiting for a prolonged period.
idle_gaps: 02:15 - 04:19 (2 minutes 04 seconds) - Static screen on error page, no mouse or keyboard activity.
tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician seemed methodical in identifying the error and then searching for a solution. However, the long idle period after the error might indicate a momentary pause in the troubleshooting process, possibly due to uncertainty or waiting.
coaching_flags: Review the long idle period (2 minutes 04 seconds) after the error message appeared. Encourage more proactive steps or communication during such delays to maintain efficiency.
